content.question_stem: Which cloud computing model delivers fully managed software applications over the internet on a subscription basis?

options.explanation: IaaS provides virtualized computing resources, not fully managed applications.
PaaS provides a framework for developers to build applications.
SaaS delivers fully hosted, ready-to-use software applications over the internet.
DaaS provides virtual desktop environments.

content.question_stem: An organization needs a disaster recovery site that is fully equipped with hardware and exact copies of data, capable of taking over processing within minutes. Which site should they utilize?

options.explanation: A cold site has space and power but no hardware or immediate data.
A warm site has hardware but requires data restoration, taking hours or days.
A hot site is fully operational with synchronized data and can take over almost immediately.
A mobile site is a portable facility, not a synchronized data center.

content.question_stem: In the Systems Development Life Cycle (SDLC), during which phase is user acceptance testing (UAT) typically performed?

options.explanation: Requirements definition occurs before any coding or testing.
Development involves writing the actual code.
UAT is the final part of the Testing phase before the system moves to implementation.
Maintenance occurs after the system is already live.

content.question_stem: Which of the following represents a failure in proper segregation of duties within IT change management?

options.text: A developer writes code and an IT manager approves the deployment.
options.explanation: This is a proper segregation of duties.

options.text: A systems administrator deploys code that was tested by QA.
options.explanation: This is acceptable as long as the admin did not write the code.

options.text: A developer writes the code and moves it directly into the production environment.
options.explanation: Developers should never have access to deploy their own code into production.

options.text: A business user requests a change and performs User Acceptance Testing.
options.explanation: Business users requesting and testing changes is standard practice.

content.question_stem: What is the primary function of an Application Programming Interface (API)?

options.text: To encrypt data resting in a database.
options.explanation: Encryption is handled by cryptographic algorithms, not APIs.

options.text: To allow two distinct software systems to communicate and share data.
options.explanation: APIs act as bridges that let different applications talk to one another.
Blocking traffic is the function of a firewall.

options.text: To serve as the primary interface for human users.
options.explanation: Graphical User Interfaces (GUIs) are for humans; APIs are for software.

content.question_stem: An auditor notes that a company relies on a third-party software vendor for a critical application. Which of the following mitigates the risk of the vendor going out of business?

options.explanation: SLAs mandate performance metrics but don't protect against bankruptcy.
Escrow ensures the client receives the source code if the vendor ceases operations.
NDAs protect confidentiality, not business continuity.
A BIA identifies risks but does not directly mitigate the loss of vendor code.

content.question_stem: What does Recovery Time Objective (RTO) define in business continuity planning?

options.text: The maximum amount of data loss an organization can tolerate.
options.explanation: This describes the Recovery Point Objective (RPO).

options.text: The maximum acceptable amount of time a system can be offline after a disaster.
options.explanation: RTO is the target time set for the resumption of operations after an incident.

options.text: The time it takes to perform a full system backup.
options.explanation: Backup duration is an operational metric, not RTO.

options.text: The exact time an outage occurred.
options.explanation: This is simply an incident timestamp.

content.question_stem: Which of the following is an example of an IT General Control (ITGC)?

options.text: A three-way match configured in an accounts payable system.
options.explanation: A three-way match is an automated application control, not an ITGC.

options.text: A system setting that requires all passwords to be at least 12 characters long.
options.explanation: Logical access controls (like password policies) are foundational IT General Controls.

options.text: A mathematical validation check on a data entry form.
options.explanation: Input validation is an application-level control.

options.text: A mandatory review of a specific journal entry.
options.explanation: This is a manual business process control.

content.question_stem: Which technology allows multiple operating systems to run simultaneously on a single physical server?

options.explanation: Containerization isolates applications, but they share a single OS kernel.
Virtualization uses a hypervisor to run multiple isolated operating systems on one machine.
Load balancing distributes network traffic across multiple servers.
Microservices is an architectural style for developing applications.

content.question_stem: An organization is implementing an Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) system. What is the primary advantage of this architecture?

options.text: It guarantees immunity from cybersecurity breaches.
options.explanation: No system is immune to cyber attacks.

options.text: It integrates disparate business functions into a single, centralized database.
options.explanation: ERPs eliminate data silos by combining finance, HR, and supply chain data into one system.

options.text: It eliminates the need for IT General Controls.
options.explanation: ERPs require strict ITGCs to function securely.

options.text: It relies exclusively on decentralized blockchain ledgers.
options.explanation: Traditional ERPs use centralized relational databases.

blueprint_area: Area 2: Data Management & Data Life Cycle

content.question_stem: In the context of data management, what does the acronym ETL stand for?

options.explanation: This is a made-up security term.
ETL is the process of pulling data, cleaning it, and loading it into a data warehouse.
This refers to cybersecurity analysis.
This is a fictitious term.

blueprint_area: Area 2: Data Management & Data Life Cycle

content.question_stem: In a relational database, what is the purpose of a foreign key?

options.explanation: Foreign keys are related to data architecture, not encryption.

options.text: To uniquely identify each record within a single table.
options.explanation: This is the definition of a Primary Key.

options.text: To establish a link between the data in two different tables.
options.explanation: A foreign key in one table points to a primary key in another to create a relationship.

options.text: To prevent unauthorized users from viewing the table.
options.explanation: This is handled by access controls, not foreign keys.

blueprint_area: Area 2: Data Management & Data Life Cycle

content.question_stem: Which document acts as a centralized repository containing definitions, structures, and metadata about the data elements in a database?

options.explanation: A data dictionary provides detailed information about data types, lengths, and relationships.
An SLA governs vendor performance.
This maps hardware and network paths, not metadata.
A SOC 2 reports on controls, not database metadata.

blueprint_area: Area 2: Data Management & Data Life Cycle

content.question_stem: Which of the following is considered unstructured data?

options.text: An Excel spreadsheet of inventory counts.
options.explanation: Spreadsheets are highly structured.

options.text: A SQL database containing customer addresses.
options.explanation: Relational databases hold structured data.

options.text: A folder full of recorded customer service phone calls.
options.explanation: Audio, video, and free-form emails lack a predefined data model, making them unstructured.

options.text: A CSV file of daily transaction totals.
options.explanation: CSV files are structured formats.

blueprint_area: Area 2: Data Management & Data Life Cycle

content.question_stem: An organization moves historical transaction data that is rarely accessed from its primary production database to a cheaper, long-term storage facility. Which phase of the data life cycle does this represent?

options.explanation: Creation is the initial generation of data.
Archiving involves moving inactive data to long-term storage for compliance or retention.
Destruction implies permanently deleting the data.
Processing is the active manipulation of data.

blueprint_area: Area 2: Data Management & Data Life Cycle

content.question_stem: What is a defining characteristic of a Data Lake compared to a Data Warehouse?

options.text: A data lake stores massive amounts of raw, unstructured data.
options.explanation: Data lakes hold raw data in its native format, whereas data warehouses hold structured, transformed data.

options.text: A data lake only accepts strictly relational SQL tables.
options.explanation: Data lakes are designed for non-relational and unstructured data.

options.text: A data lake automatically deletes data after 30 days.
options.explanation: Data lakes are generally for long-term storage.

options.text: A data lake is only hosted on on-premise mainframes.
options.explanation: Data lakes are frequently hosted in the cloud (e.g., AWS S3).

blueprint_area: Area 2: Data Management & Data Life Cycle

content.question_stem: Which discipline focuses on creating a single, accurate, and authoritative point of reference for an organization's critical business data (e.g., standardizing 'Customer Name' across all systems)?

options.explanation: MDM ensures uniformity and accuracy of critical shared data across the enterprise.
Change management deals with software updates.
This relates to cryptography.
Agile is a software development methodology.

blueprint_area: Area 2: Data Management & Data Life Cycle

content.question_stem: To ensure compliance with data governance policies, who is primarily responsible for classifying a set of data and determining who should have access to it?

options.explanation: IT administrators implement the access controls, but do not determine the business rules.
